meta.title: Wave 4 — Clawhip Absorption Pass
meta.description: 11 features. 4 absorb into skills, 4 absorb into lev-tmux Wave 3, 1 reference, 2 reject.

props.title: Edge Router Absorption
props.subtitle: clawhip is infrastructure, not a skill pack. 4 patterns absorb into skills now; 4 absorb into the lev-tmux Rust crate (Wave 3); 2 rejected; 1 reference only.

props.content: clawhip's comparison verdict is **extract** — take the edge-router and event-contract ideas, reject it as Lev's runtime center. The session.* event normalization, renderer/sink separation, hook-envelope bridge, and tmux monitoring patterns are the targets. The Discord/Slack sinks and daemon-product specifics stay behind. Most of the real value (prompt delivery, content hashing, circuit breaker, session registry) goes into the lev-tmux Rust crate in Wave 3.

props.title: 🎯 Absorb → Skills (4)
props.content:
**cw-01** session.* event normalization → `/lev` event bus docs
**cw-02** Dispatcher → Router → Renderer → Sink → `/arch` pipeline pattern
**cw-03** OMC/OMX hook-envelope bridge → `/lev-intake` hook bridge
**cw-04** tmux stale/keyword alerts → `/autodev-lev` stale heuristics

props.title: 🦀 Absorb → lev-tmux (4)
props.content:
**cw-05** Prompt delivery with TUI readiness + retry + verify
**cw-06** Circuit breaker with DLQ + rate limit + timer wheel
**cw-07** Content hash for change detection
**cw-08** Shared session registry (Arc<RwLock<HashMap>>)

These are Rust code patterns, not skill doc patterns. They go into the vendor crate.

props.title: 📋 Reference + Reject (3)
props.content:
**cw-09** Daemon systemd integration → **reference** (different architecture than core/daemon TS)
**cw-10** Memory scaffolds → **reject** (Lev already stronger)
**cw-11** Discord/Slack sinks → **reject** (stripped in vendor plan)

rows.feature: tmux stale/keyword alerts + content hash monitoring
Prompt delivery: TUI readiness + retry + content-hash verify
Circuit breaker + DLQ + rate limit + timer wheel
Content hash for tmux pane change detection
Shared session registry with parent process tracking

props.title: 📡 → /lev (event bus)
props.content:
**Absorb:** session.* event normalization contract

clawhip maps legacy `agent.started/finished/failed` → normalized `session.started/completed/error`. The normalization doctrine is the pattern, not the code.

**Section to add:** 'Session Event Normalization (from clawhip session.* contract)'

props.title: 🏗️ → /arch (pipeline pattern)
props.content:
**Absorb:** 4-stage delivery pipeline: Dispatcher → Router → Renderer → Sink

The renderer/sink split is what Lev's event bus lacks. Events route to a renderer (format for human consumption), then to a sink (deliver). Lev collapses these today.

**Section to add:** 'Edge Delivery Pipeline (from clawhip renderer/sink separation)'

props.title: 🔌 → /lev-intake (hook bridge)
props.content:
**Absorb:** OMC/OMX hook-envelope bridge pattern

`clawhip omx hook` and `/api/omx/hook` forward hook events between runtimes. The envelope wraps: source_agent, event_type, payload, timestamp, session_id.

**Section to add:** 'Hook-Envelope Bridge (from clawhip OMC/OMX bridge)'

props.title: 🔍 → /autodev-lev (stale detection)
props.content:
**Absorb:** tmux session monitoring heuristics

clawhip's stale detection: keyword windows (aggregate hits over N seconds), parent process tracking, content hash comparison for drift, configurable stale_minutes per session.

**Section to add:** 'Stale Session Detection (from clawhip tmux monitoring)'

These 4 features are **code patterns**, not skill doc patterns. They go into the lev-tmux Rust crate when we vendor clawhip:

- **cw-05** Prompt delivery — TUI readiness detection, send-keys -l, content-hash verify, retry (3 attempts default, 500ms poll, 250ms verify delay)
- **cw-06** Circuit breaker — clawhip's core/circuit_breaker.rs is more sophisticated than heartbeat.ts: dead-letter queue, rate limiting via core/rate_limit.rs, timer wheel via core/timer_wheel.rs
- **cw-07** Content hash — `content_hash()` in source/tmux.rs. DefaultHasher on capture-pane output. Compare before/after send to verify delivery.
- **cw-08** Session registry — `SharedTmuxRegistry = Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, RegisteredTmuxSession>>>`. Tracks: session name, channel, keywords, stale_minutes, format, registration source, parent process.

All 4 are already documented in `.lev/scratch/feature-dna-drafts/clawhip-vendor.dna.yaml`.

props.title: cw-10: Memory scaffolds
props.content: clawhip uses repo-local MEMORY.md + shards for operational memory. Lev already has .lev/pm/ + core/memory/ which is structurally richer. No absorption needed.

props.title: cw-11: Discord/Slack sinks
props.content: Product-specific delivery sinks. Explicitly stripped in clawhip-vendor.dna.yaml. Lev stays surface-agnostic above the sink edge.

props.title: 2. Wave 3 plan already exists
props.content: clawhip-vendor.dna.yaml documents the 4 Rust crate items. plan-clawhip-vendor-integration.md is filed. Blocked on A1 arch interview for placement.
